articles.title: Zero-day exploit completely defeats default Windows 11 BitLocker protections
articles.description: It's not entirely clear how the exploit works. Microsoft says it's investigating.

articles.title: Cisco announces record revenue and 4,000 layoffs in the same day
articles.description: Layoffs are "not a savings-driven restructure," CFO says.

articles.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
articles.description: Production-version patches are coming online and should be installed pronto.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.description: Across the country, schools and colleges postpone year-end tests.

summaries: A zero-day exploit has been discovered that can bypass default Windows 11 BitLocker protections, with Microsoft currently investigating the issue. The exploit's method of operation is unclear. This vulnerability poses a significant risk to users relying on BitLocker for data encryption.

Cisco has announced record revenue, but simultaneously disclosed plans to lay off 4,000 employees. The company's CFO stated that the layoffs are not driven by cost-cutting measures, but rather a strategic decision. The exact reasons behind the layoffs remain unclear.

Linux has been affected by a severe vulnerability, marking the second significant issue in as many weeks. Production-version patches are being released and are recommended to be installed promptly to mitigate potential risks. Users are advised to update their systems to prevent potential security breaches.

A cyberattack on the learning platform Canvas has disrupted educational institutions nationwide, forcing many to postpone year-end tests. The attack has caused widespread disruption and chaos, with schools and colleges scrambling to find alternative solutions.
technology.brief:
- A zero-day exploit has been discovered that can bypass Windows 11 BitLocker protections, with Microsoft investigating.
- Cisco has announced record revenue alongside plans to lay off 4,000 employees, citing a strategic decision rather than cost-cutting.
- Linux has been hit by a severe vulnerability, with production-version patches available for installation to prevent potential security breaches.
- A cyberattack on Canvas has disrupted educational institutions nationwide, forcing many to postpone year-end tests.
